faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: We've answered the most common questions about GetInvestr for you. Everything from platform capabilities to security is detailed below.
faq.q1: What exactly is GetInvestr and who is it for?
faq.a1: GetInvestr is a comprehensive investor database and CRM platform exclusively designed for entrepreneurs and start-up founders. With thousands of angel investors and VC funds, you can securely filter the best matches for your startup and manage all your communications from a single hub.
faq.q2: How often is your database updated?
faq.a2: Our database is updated weekly through both automated scans and manual verification by our team to ensure new investors are added and existing information remains accurate.
faq.q3: How do I contact the investors?
faq.a3: You can view the email address and LinkedIn profile of any investor right on the platform to reach out directly from your own email. Alternatively, you can use our integrated CRM system to send your first message / pitch directly within the platform and manage the entire process from a single hub.
faq.q4: How does the '3 months free' on yearly plans work?
faq.a4: When you choose a yearly subscription, you pay for only 9 months of usage instead of 12. This effectively applies a 3-month discount to your billing total. The credits included in your chosen plan will continue to be refilled into your account every month.
faq.q5: Which regions are covered by the investors on the platform?
faq.a5: GetInvestr's database covers a global network. You can access thousands of investor profiles from the US (Silicon Valley), Europe, the MENA region, and Turkey.
faq.q6: How can I pitch my startup better?
faq.a6: Thanks to our in-platform resources and the Outreach AI feature, you can access powerful templates and guidance to ensure your messages to investors make a great impression.
faq.q7: Is my credit card information secure?
faq.a7: Absolutely. Our payment infrastructure is entirely secured by Stripe, and your credit card information is never stored on our servers.

blog.title: Latest Venture Publications
blog.subtitle: The most recent seed investment news, deep-dive ecosystem analytics, and founder guides.

posts.title: Turkish Startup Ecosystem Raised $1.4 Billion in 2025
posts.content:
According to the newly published 'Turkey Startup Investments' report in collaboration with KPMG Turkey and 212, the Turkish startup ecosystem successfully raised a total of $1.4 billion across 360 investments in 2025.

Despite the absence of mega-rounds or newly minted decacorns, this figure once again proved the resilience and strong growth potential of the market. Experts highlight that while investors have become more selective during periods of global economic tightening, interest in startups with solid, proven business models remains unwavering.

The ecosystem is witnessing continued activity, especially in early-stage (seed) investments. Platforms like GetInvestr are expected to further accelerate early-stage growth by bridging the gap between innovative founders and active angel networks or VCs globally.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: Mega Growth in Fintech: $214 Million Investment in Turkey
posts.content:
Financial technology (Fintech) continues to be the locomotive of the investment ecosystem in Turkey. According to recent data, fintech startups attracted $214 million in funding last year, ranking among the top verticals.

This maturity level reflects investors' growing interest in digital wallets, embedded finance, B2B payment solutions, and regulatory technologies (regtech). Supported by open banking regulations, the number of innovative players is scaling fast across the market.

Foreign VCs' engagement with Turkish fintech is also snowballing. GetInvestr data reveals that interactions between global investment funds and Turkey-based fintech companies have doubled over the past year.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: Global AI Funding Peaks in March 2026: The Rise of 'Agentic' Technologies
posts.content:
As we close the first quarter of 2026, the Artificial Intelligence frenzy continues unabated in the global VC landscape. In the final week of March alone, startups focusing on AI infrastructure raised millions.

Standout seed investments included AI personal memory platform Littlebird ($11M), AI-native Human Capital Management platform Novaworks ($8M), and AI design agent Moda ($7.5M).

Investors are fundamentally shifting their thesis: Rather than funding startups that simply bolt AI features onto existing products, capital is aggressively flowing toward core technologies utilizing AI to automate end-to-end workflows (AI Agents) and disrupt traditional paradigms.
posts.author: GetInvestr Research

posts.title: Cleantech Surtge: Helix Earth Secures $12 Million Seed Funding
posts.content:
Sustainability and carbon reduction goals are creating lucrative new opportunities in the startup ecosystem, shifting the VC radar heavily toward Energy and Climate Tech (Cleantech).

In one of March's largest seed rounds, Helix Earth secured $12 million in an oversubscribed Seed 2 round to transform HVAC energy efficiency.

While hardware-focused startups are generally perceived as harder to fund compared to SaaS, 'DeepTech' ventures tackling the severe climate crisis with tangible solutions are breaking funding records. The growing emphasis on ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) criteria ensures that capital allocated to cleantech and energy startups will multiply in the coming quarters.
posts.author: GetInvestr Research

posts.title: Winning Tactics for AI Startup Fundraising
posts.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a 'hype'; it has become an integral part of business processes. However, building an AI startup is quite costly due to high GPU costs and the need for qualified data.

Things you should pay attention to to impress AI investors:

- Have a differentiated dataset. You cannot make a difference with data that everyone can access.

- Focus on the concrete value this model offers to the end user, not just the AI model.

- Prove the depth of your technical team. Investors look at the capacity to develop this algorithm.

Investors expect you to say 'we solve this problem faster than ever before with AI', not just 'we use AI too'.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: 2024 Investment Outlook for B2B SaaS Startups
posts.content:
The SaaS (Software as a Service) world has entered a new era focused on efficiency and profitability. Investors are now looking for operational sustainability as well as fast growth.

Prominent SaaS trends of 2024:

- Product-Led Growth (PLG): Models where the product sells itself without establishing a large sales team are considered more valuable.

- Low Churn: SaaS that manage to keep their customers inside are always one step ahead.

- Integration Capability: In today's complex technology stack, SaaS that can talk fluently with other tools are preferred.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: Top 10 Fastest Growing Turkish Fintech Startups Securing Funds in 2024
posts.content:
The Turkish fintech ecosystem continued its growth in the first half of 2024 despite global economic fluctuations. In particular, investments received by giants like Colendi and Midas show how hungry the market still is. Here are the 10 most talked-about fintech startups of 2024:

1. Colendi: At the top of the list with its global vision in digital banking and 'buy now pay later' (BNPL) services.

2. Midas: A platform that democratizes access to American stock markets and BIST for local investors, it closed a massive funding round with an aggressive increase in the number of users.

3. Param: It is taking firm steps towards becoming one of Turkey's most valuable fintechs with its payment systems and wallet solutions.

4. Papara: Reaching millions with its user experience-oriented financial services, the startup continues its strategy of opening up to global markets.

5. Figopara: It has become an indispensable partner for SMEs with its supplier financing solutions that help businesses manage their cash flow.

6. United Payment: One of the strongest players in the region with its 'invisible finance' solutions offered to corporate companies.

7. Craftgate: With its online payment gateway solutions, it allows managing the complex payment processes of the e-commerce world from a single point.

8. Sipay: One of the fast-growing fintech stars with its innovative approaches in wallet, card, and payment systems.

9. Pay TR: As a payment institution, it crowns its stability for many years by strengthening its technological infrastructure with new investments.

10. Hadi (Fuat): An innovative startup that spreads digital financial services to the base with market chain integrations.

These investments strengthen Turkey's vision of becoming a regional fintech hub day by day.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: Top 3 Mistakes When Preparing for Series A
posts.content:
The Series A round is the time for a start-up to turn into a real and corporate enterprise. Many founders cannot complete the process due to the following critical mistakes in this round:

1. Not Being Data-Driven: In Series A, you are expected to talk with deeply analyzed Excel tables, not just dreams.

2. Valuation Stubbornness: Closing a round with a very high valuation brings the risk of a 'Down Round' in the next round and extreme dilution of the founder.

3. Not Completing the Team: Investors now expect middle and upper level managers to be in place outside the founder team.

Avoiding these mistakes and preparing a professional Data Room greatly increases your chances of successfully closing the round.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: Critical Metrics You Need to Prepare Before Securing a Pre-Seed Fund Round
posts.content:
The pre-seed stage is the most challenging process when an idea turns into a concrete product. A pitch deck alone is not always enough for investors to believe in you. Here are the 3 main metrics you should focus on:

1. Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC): How much do you spend per unit to sell your product? It is essential that this figure is sustainable.

2. Customer Lifetime Value (LTV): The total value a customer earns you should be at least 3 times higher than the acquisition cost.

3. Retention Rate: Do your users come back after first use? If not, you should reconsider your product-market fit.

Consistency among these metrics is your strongest proof of the long-term success of your business model.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

posts.title: Actively Searching For HealthTech? Connect with These Top Tier Angel Investors
posts.content:
The healthcare sector is undergoing a major transformation under the influence of AI and digitalization. Successful examples in Turkey like Salus and SpiroHome have brought investor interest in this area to its peak.

Main networks and focuses actively investing in HealthTech:

- Galata Business Angels (GBA): Attaches great importance to digital health solutions with its wide portal.

- Keiretsu Forum Turkey: Opens local health startups to the world with its global network advantage.

- TR Angel: Provides cash support and mentoring in early-stage health technology startups.

The most important criteria for these investors are clinical validation of the product, patient safety, and global scalability.
posts.author: GetInvestr Team

pages.aboutContent:
GetInvestr is an innovative financial technology platform aiming to bridge the gap between startup founders, angel investors, venture capitalists, and corporate investors.

Our objective is to dramatically minimize the months-long investment sourcing process, enabling the fastest and most secure direct matchmaking. We build our technology with passion, continuing to empower the startup ecosystem universally.
pages.contactContent:
Do not hesitate to reach out! For any questions or suggestions, please contact us through the following address:

Email: info@getinvestr.com

Our team will get back to you immediately.
pages.careersContent:
We operate with a transparent career vision and a hunger for innovation.

We do not have any open positions at the moment. However, if you would like to join our vibrant team or be considered for future opportunities, please forward your resume to careers@investr.ai. We'd love to meet you!
pages.termsContent:
By utilizing this platform, you acknowledge and agree to abide by all rulings of GetInvestr.

Registered users commit to maintaining accurate information securely. Content validation and materials provided are your own responsibility.

Important Reminder: Whether an investor decides to respond to your communication is entirely up to the investor themselves. GetInvestr assumes no responsibility or liability if an investor does not respond to your outreach.

We reserve the full rights to alter our global terms at any moment here exclusively.
pages.privacyContent:
We strictly value your online privacy.

Your personal data and account identifiers are heavily encrypted under ultimate structural security protocols. Your information is never shared openly with unauthorized third-parties or external advertising domains.
pages.cookieContent:
Cookies are persistently utilized on our platform to guarantee you an extensively better overall experience.

Embedded cookies are primarily geared towards global site diagnostic analytics, functionality personalization, and strict internal security criteria matching. By continuing to navigate across our portal, you formally accept these embedded rulesets.
